final_rules.instructions: This rule detects common SQL injection patterns used for authentication bypass or query termination. It normalizes the input by applying two rounds of URL decoding and converting to lowercase, then looks for a quote followed by a SQL comment initiator or statement terminator.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidates multiple rules detecting encoded quotes ('%2527', '%252527') and terminators ('--+', '--%2b', '%252b--') into a single, robust rule. Uses transformations (urlDecodeUni x2, lowercase) to normalize input, eliminating the need for separate case and encoding-specific patterns. Covers payloads like 'admin%2527%252b--'.
final_rules.rule: SecRule REQUEST_URI|REQUEST_BODY|ARGS "@rx (?:'|\"|´)\s*(?:--|#|;)" "id:942001,phase:2,block,t:none,t:urlDecodeUni,t:urlDecodeUni,t:lowercase,msg:'SQL Injection: Authentication Bypass or Query Truncation Attempt',tag:'attack-sqli',ver:'CUSTOM/1.0',severity:'CRITICAL'"

final_rules.instructions: This rule targets the high-confidence `UNION SELECT` SQLi pattern. It applies aggressive normalization, including removing SQL comments (`/**/`, `--`) and compressing whitespace, which neuters many common evasion techniques before pattern matching.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Merges over a dozen individual rules for 'union', 'select', case variations, and comment/whitespace bypasses (`%250a`, `%2509`, `/*`). The use of `t:removeComments` and `t:compressWhitespace` makes the simple regex pattern extremely effective against obfuscated payloads like '%2527/%252A%252a/UNiOn/%252a%252A/SElEcT/...'

final_rules.instructions: Detects function calls used for time-based blind SQL injection. The pattern is normalized to catch various encodings of the function name and parenthesis.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidates multiple 'sleep' rules (e.g., 'sleep(', 'sleep%28', 'SLeEp%2528') into one pattern by using normalization. Expanded to include other common time-based blind functions (`benchmark`, `pg_sleep`, `waitfor delay`) for broader coverage.

final_rules.instructions: Detects SQL tautologies like 'OR 1=1' which are used to bypass logical checks. The regex is generalized to catch variations like 'OR 'a'='a'.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Replaces multiple specific 'or 1=1' rules, including those for different encodings (`%2520`, `%253d`). The refined regex is more generic, detecting `[something]=[something]` preceded by `or` or `and`, which is more resilient to evasion than a static '1=1' check. Covers '1%2520oR%25201%253d1'.

final_rules.instructions: This rule looks for highly specific, anomalous strings found in the bypassed payloads. These are artifacts of obfuscation tools or polyglot (SQLi+XSS) payloads and have a very low false positive rate.
final_rules.refinement_notes: Consolidates several rules targeting unique, high-confidence indicators into a single regex. Adds `t:htmlEntityDecode` to handle payloads like '...PArT%2520oN&%2347%3bAtioNaveRsion...'. The pattern targets the decoded, lowercase form of these artifacts for maximum efficiency.
final_rules.rule: SecRule REQUEST_URI|REQUEST_BODY|ARGS "@rx (?:alert\(|atimeend|aversion|part\s+on|ne1iptext|fromthemaykey)" "id:942005,phase:2,block,t:none,t:urlDecodeUni,t:urlDecodeUni,t:htmlEntityDecode,t:lowercase,msg:'SQL Injection: Advanced Evasion/Polyglot Artifacts Detected',tag:'attack-sqli',ver:'CUSTOM/1.0',severity:'CRITICAL'"

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects double and triple URL-encoded single quotes (%2527 = %25 + 27 = decoded single quote, %2525 = double-encoded percent). These are the primary obfuscation carrier across all clusters. Covers the outer encoding layer before normalization. Apply to URI and query string. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri contains "%2527" or http.request.uri contains "%25%27" or http.request.uri contains "%2525" or http.request.uri.query contains "%2527" or http.request.uri.query contains "%25%27" or http.request.uri.query contains "%2525")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Catches triple and quadruple URL-encoded apostrophes seen in Cluster 1 and Cluster 0. Attackers encode %25 -> % repeatedly to evade single-pass decoders. Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "%252527" or http.request.uri.query contains "%25252527" or http.request.uri contains "%252527" or http.request.uri contains "%25252527" or http.request.body contains "%252527" or http.request.body contains "%25252527")
validation_warnings: Unknown field: http.request.body (may be a newer Cloudflare field)

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Case-insensitive UNION keyword detection in query strings after Cloudflare applies its built-in normalization. Covers Clusters 4, 6, 7 UNION-based SQL injection. Cloudflare normalizes percent-encoding before field evaluation in many cases. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK. Note: Cloudflare Free plan field matching is case-sensitive with 'contains'; include common mixed-case variants manually.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "union" or http.request.uri.query contains "uNion" or http.request.uri.query contains "UNion" or http.request.uri.query contains "UNION" or http.request.uri.query contains "UniON" or http.request.uri.query contains "uNIon" or http.request.uri.query contains "uNION" or http.request.uri.query contains "UNIoN")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Case-variant SELECT keyword detection covering Clusters 1, 2, 4, 6. Paired with UNION rule to catch UNION SELECT chains. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects double and triple encoded tab characters (%2509 = %09 = tab, %252509 = triple encoded) used in Clusters 1, 3, 7 to replace spaces between SQL keywords. Tabs in URL query parameters are highly anomalous for legitimate use. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "%2509" or http.request.uri.query contains "%25090" or http.request.uri.query contains "%252509" or http.request.uri.query contains "%25252509" or http.request.uri contains "%2509" or http.request.uri contains "%252509" or http.request.uri contains "%25252509")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects time-based blind SQL injection via SLEEP() function targeting Cluster 2. Covers raw and URL-encoded parentheses. Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "sleep(" or http.request.uri.query contains "sleep%28" or http.request.uri.query contains "SLeEp%28" or http.request.uri.query contains "SLEEP%28" or http.request.uri.query contains "SLeEp(" or http.request.uri.query contains "Sleep(" or http.request.uri.query contains "SLEEP(")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects AND+SLEEP and AND+SELECT combinations characteristic of boolean-based and time-based blind injection from Clusters 1 and 2. Combining two weak signals into a stronger compound rule reduces false positives. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects error-based SQL injection via UPDATEXML and CONCAT with hex literals from Cluster 2. These functions are rarely used in legitimate query parameters. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "updatexml" or http.request.uri.query contains "UpdateXmL" or http.request.uri.query contains "UPDATEXML" or http.request.uri.query contains "updateXML" or http.request.uri.query contains "concat(0x" or http.request.uri.query contains "concat%280x" or http.request.uri.query contains "concat%25280x")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects SQL/MySQL inline comment injection (/* */ and /*!...*/) used extensively in Clusters 1 and 4 to break up keywords and evade detection. The %252a pattern is double-encoded asterisk. Legitimate query strings do not contain SQL comment syntax. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "/*" or http.request.uri.query contains "/*!" or http.request.uri.query contains "/**" or http.request.uri.query contains "%252a" or http.request.uri.query contains "%252A" or http.request.uri.query contains "%2f%2a" or http.request.uri.query contains "%2F%2A")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Targets double-encoded comment delimiters %252f%252a (= %2f%2a = /*) from Clusters 1 and 4. These are used to fragment SQL keywords across comment blocks. Severity: CRITICAL. Action: BLOCK.
generated_rules.rule: (http.request.uri.query contains "%252f%252a" or http.request.uri.query contains "%252F%252A" or http.request.uri.query contains "%252f%252A" or http.request.uri.query contains "%252F%252a" or http.request.uri contains "%252f%252a" or http.request.uri contains "%252F%252A")

generated_rules.instructions: BLOCK - Detects SQL comment terminators (-- variants) used to truncate queries in Clusters 6, 7. %2b/%2B = plus sign which URL-decodes in some contexts, -- followed by space is standard SQL comment. Severity: HIGH. Action: BLOCK.
